body.title: Post-response filter: block bare acknowledgment phrases before channel post
body.result: Already fully implemented in main.py: _is_bare_ack() + _BARE_ACK_PATTERNS regex filter applied pre-channel-post. Tests in test_deadend_ack_filter.py.
body.description: Igor responded Fair. On it. to akien despite system_prompt.py line 142-143 explicitly banning it. Root cause: tier.2 (small Ollama model) does not reliably follow nuanced system_prompt constraints. Fix strategy: add a post-generation filter in main.py response pipeline. Before posting an LLM response to channel, check if it matches bare-ack patterns: [Fair. On it., Got it., On it., Understood., Will do., etc.]. If match: suppress the response entirely (the action IS the response). Alternative: reinforce via a habit that fires on conversation-closure signals and emits a structured response instead of a bare ack. Note: system prompt fix is insufficient — tier.2 ignores it. Need code-level enforcement. Files: main.py (post-LLM, pre-post filter). Inertia: MEDIUM.
